summary: Fixes an IDOR vulnerability where account ownership was not verified.
Fixes improper authorization by replacing a generic login check with a staff-specific requirement.
Introduces an unauthenticated endpoint exposing sensitive environment variables.
Introduces an API endpoint lacking server-side authorization, relying on client-side hiding.
Enforces server-side authorization on an API endpoint to prevent unauthorized access.
Introduces hardcoded default credentials and insecure sample applications into production.
Prevents deployment with default insecure credentials and keys.
Disables directory browsing to prevent information disclosure.
Exposes sensitive stack traces and internal system information to the client.
Implements secure storage with least-privilege access and short-lived signed URLs.
Introduces a compromised dependency containing malicious code.
Mitigates supply chain risks by vendoring and signing core dependencies.
Introduces a library version with a known RCE vulnerability.
Weakens password hashing by replacing secure algorithms with unsalted SHA1.
Proposes using MD5 for password hashing, which is cryptographically broken.
Upgrades password hashing to Argon2id for better resistance against modern attacks.
Introduces SQL injection via raw SQL queries using string formatting.
Replaces unsafe raw SQL with parameterized ORM queries.
Introduces OS command injection via unsanitized input in subprocess calls.
Introduces insecure knowledge-based authentication (security questions) for recovery.
Replaces insecure recovery methods with cryptographically secure one-time codes.
Introduces a logic flaw allowing resource exhaustion (DoS) via unvalidated booking requests.
Implements anti-bot and rate-limiting logic to prevent resource abuse.
Introduces an authentication endpoint vulnerable to brute-force and username enumeration.
Implements MFA to mitigate credential stuffing and unauthorized access.
Introduces session management flaws by disabling server-side logout.
Implements robust session management, including absolute timeouts and cross-app logout.
Introduces an authentication bypass vulnerability by trusting unverified headers.

summary: Enforces cryptographic signature verification for remote assets.

summary: Introduces RCE vulnerability by using insecure Pickle serialization for client-side state.

owasp_category: A08: Software or Data Integrity Failures
summary: Enforces secure JSON serialization to prevent deserialization attacks.

summary: Disables critical audit logging, preventing detection of data exfiltration.

summary: Removes logging of failed logins, hindering brute-force detection.

summary: Implements structured logging and alerting for brute-force detection.

summary: Implements comprehensive audit logging for real-time threat detection.

Sanitizes error responses to prevent information leakage during exceptions.
